hindi: यू तियाओ (चीनी तली हुई आटे की छड़ी)

region_of_origin: China; universally adopted across Singapore's Chinese, Malay, and Indian communities
food_subgroup: Deep-fried leavened wheat flour dough sticks

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no dedicated concept for You Tiao. Closest parent is a deep-fried leavened wheat flour dough product. No URI assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Deep-fried leavened wheat flour dough sticks (Chinese you tiao / crullers)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0CEB is the closest FoodEx2 parent for deep-fried leavened wheat dough products; no exact code exists for you tiao specifically.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept identified for you tiao or Chinese fried dough sticks.

main_ingredients: leavening agent (baking powder or ammonium bicarbonate)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard you tiao stick weighs approximately 60 g and is the typical single-serve portion sold at kopitiams and hawker stalls across Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: You Tiao / Dough Fritters — Health Promotion Board Singapore Food Composition Database (HPB Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Singapore Foodo lists you tiao / dough fritters as a named entry. All per-100g nutrient values are taken directly from this source. Confidence 1.0 for all macros and micronutrients as reported in the input record.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Refined Wheat Flour Deep-Fried Dough Products — University of Sydney GI Database (analogous estimate: refined wheat flour-based deep-fried dough, GI ~75–90)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for you tiao exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 78 is estimated from the closest analogous food: refined wheat flour deep-fried dough products (GI range 75–90 per University of Sydney GI Database). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (78 × 21.9) / 100 = 17.1, rounded to 17.0. Serving = 60 g stick providing 21.9 g carbohydrate (36.5 g/100g × 0.60). Deep-frying fat provides modest GI moderation but the high-GI refined flour base dominates.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content 480 mg per 100 g; one 60 g stick delivers approximately 288 mg sodium
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit you tiao to one stick per meal occasion and avoid pairing it with other high-sodium foods such as salted soya bean milk or preserved vegetables, as the cumulative sodium load can approach or exceed 600 mg per breakfast sitting.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~78) from refined wheat flour base; glycemic load of 17.0 per 60 g stick
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should limit consumption to one you tiao stick per breakfast and avoid combining it with other high-GI carbohydrate foods such as plain congee or sweetened soya bean milk, as the combined glycemic load can cause a significant postprandial glucose spike.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat 17.8 g/100g including saturated fat 3.2 g and trans fat 0.2 g from commercial deep-frying; high sodium 480 mg/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit you tiao to occasional consumption due to the combined burden of saturated fat, trans fat from commercial frying oils, and moderate-high sodium, all of which are independent risk factors for adverse cardiovascular outcomes.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High GI promotes proinflammatory glycaemic response; omega-6 polyunsaturated fats from frying oil may be proinflammatory in excess; trans fat 0.2 g/100g
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should consume you tiao in moderation, as the high-GI refined flour base and omega-6-rich frying oil may contribute to systemic inflammation, though the vitamin E content (3.5 mg/100g) from the frying oil provides some antioxidant offset.

llm_training.natural_language_description: You Tiao (油條, Yóu Tiáo) — known in Cantonese as yau ja gwei and in Malay as cakoi — are the golden, hollow, puffy deep-fried wheat dough sticks that are a universally beloved morning food across Singapore and the broader Chinese diaspora. A simple dough of wheat flour, water, salt, and a leavening agent (traditionally ammonium bicarbonate or alum; modernly baking powder) is shaped into long paired strips that are joined and deep-fried in very hot vegetable oil, puffing dramatically as they cook. The inside is light, airy, and slightly chewy; the outside is golden-brown and crispy with a thin, blistered crust. You Tiao are most commonly eaten at breakfast, dipped into kopi (local coffee), warm soya bean milk, or plain rice congee. They can also be wrapped inside chee cheong fun (steamed rice noodle rolls). Nutritionally, you tiao are energy-dense at 328 kcal per 100 g, with 17.8 g fat and 36.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g. The refined wheat flour base gives a high glycemic index of approximately 78, making them a high-GI food. Sodium is moderate-high at 480 mg/100 g. Trans fat (0.2 g/100g) is present from commercial frying. Vitamin E (3.5 mg/100g) from the frying oil is a notable micronutrient. Clinically, you tiao are unsuitable for frequent consumption by individuals with type 2 diabetes due to the high glycemic load (GL 17 per stick), and should be limited by those managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease due to sodium and fat content.
common_user_queries: how many calories in one you tiao stick

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo, an approved primary source for Singapore dishes, with confidence 1.0 reported for all macros and micronutrients. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for refined wheat flour deep-fried dough products, with confidence 0.9. All mandatory multilingual names are present. Ontology uses a defensible closest-parent FoodEx2 code.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown (MUFA/PUFA split) for you tiao is not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry as of April 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo source. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo; you tiao is a plant-based deep-fried wheat dough product containing no animal-derived ingredients, so cholesterol is naturally zero. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0 — confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo; no animal or beta-carotene-rich ingredients present. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo; no fruit or vegetable ingredients present. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo; no animal-derived or fortified ingredients present. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo; you tiao contains no animal products. GI of 78 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for refined wheat flour deep-fried dough products (GI range 75–90); no direct GI measurement for you tiao exists in the database. Glycemic load per 60 g serving = (78 × 21.9) / 100 = 17.1, rounded to 17.0. Traditionally made with alum (potassium aluminium sulphate); most modern Singapore hawker stalls use baking powder instead, which affects aluminium content but not the macronutrient profile materially.
